title: Script R6: Virome Beta Diversity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b2b2j","text":"This protocol outlines the Bray-Curtis dissimilarity NMDS ordination and significance analysis of our manuscript. Here we will look at the clustering of the virome samples using our reference independent OTU table based on contig hits. We will compare skin environments (sebaceous, etc), occlusion status, and time. We also look at the difference between the background controls and the rest of the samples. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5eeb4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"60rfp","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39ml8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"449li","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fofua","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"edeeq","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8f0j1","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpgcn","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ei2nl","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7aqis","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10gj6","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fp6un","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dksbg","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library(vegan)packageVersion( library(vegan) packageVersion("vegan") library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") library(scatterplot3d) packageVersion("scatterplot3d") library(reshape2) packageVersion("reshape2") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") library(pgirmess) packageVersion("pgirmess") ## [1] '2.3.0' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '0.3.35' ## [1] '1.4.1' ## [1] '1.8.2' ## [1] '1.6.0'
Import OTU table. INPUT
Remove last column because it contains NAs (due to transposing Python script upstream). INPUT_NO_FINAL
Import mapping file. INPUT_MAP

Here we need to reformat the mapping files. This means only looking at the two time points for which we have a complete data set (we have only partial data for time point 1), as well as excluding the sites and subjects for which we only have partial sampling (as mentioned in previous notebooks).

We will be generating NMDS plots to visualize the distances between virome samples based on site environment, occlusion status, and time point. We also calculate the significance using the adonis test which was stratified across subjects.

Generate subset of mapping file for only the specific anatomic sites and all time pints (2 and 3). Remove rows in mapping file with ID of NA (meaning the mapping row belongs only to the metagenome dataset). SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[-which(INPUT_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID %in% NA), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[which(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2,3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2,3,9,11)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[c(order(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID)),]

Get only the samples described in the map subset. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_NO_FINAL[which(INPUT_NO_FINAL$ContigID %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] row.names(INPUT_SUBSET) <- INPUT_SUBSET[,1] INPUT_SUB_FORMAT <- INPUT_SUBSET[,-1] head(INPUT_SUB_FORMAT)[,c(1:5)] %s %s ## X1 X2 X4 X5 ## MG100195 0.0000 0 1.343610 2.71473 ## MG100198 0.0000 0 0.363769 0.00000 ## MG100199 0.0000 0 0.955394 0.00000 ## MG100200 25.9488 0 0.457572 2.31129 ## MG100201 0.0000 0 0.155531 0.00000 ## MG100202 50.3225 0 1.361750 5.15885
Generate distance matrix using Bray Curtis for all time point (2 and 3). INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX

Visualize the distance matrix using NMDS. BRAY_ORD_NMDS ## Run 0 stress 0.1579314 ## Run 1 stress 0.1611112 ## Run 2 stress 0.1626692 ## Run 3 stress 0.1582604 ## ... procrustes: rmse 0.03032907 max resid 0.2032916 ## Run 4 stress 0.1601818 ## Run 5 stress 0.1595255 ## Run 6 stress 0.1567941 ## ... New best solution ## ... procrustes: rmse 0.04472259 max resid 0.3011937 ## Run 7 stress 0.157843 ## Run 8 stress 0.1617624 ## Run 9 stress 0.165309 ## Run 10 stress 0.1575912 ## Run 11 stress 0.1580084 ## Run 12 stress 0.1601537 ## Run 13 stress 0.1579342 ## Run 14 stress 0.1634542 ## Run 15 stress 0.1602448 ## Run 16 stress 0.1593178 ## Run 17 stress 0.157845 ## Run 18 stress 0.1563824 ## ... New best solution ## ... procrustes: rmse 0.02635918 max resid 0.1612987 ## Run 19 stress 0.1583989 ## Run 20 stress 0.1600246

Record the stress value. BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,2], MDS3 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,3])BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,2], MDS3 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[,3]) BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress ## [1] 0.1563824

Generate and visualize merged NMDS and MAP data. BRAY_ORD_FIT$SampleID BRAY_ORD_FIT$SampleID <- rownames(BRAY_ORD_FIT) NMDS_AND_MAP <- merge(BRAY_ORD_FIT, SUBSET_MAP, by.x="SampleID", by.y="NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") head(NMDS_AND_MAP)[,c(1:5)] %s %s ## SampleID MDS1 MDS2 MDS3 NexteraXT_SampleID ## 1 MG100195 -0.039764304 0.10493926 -0.27817131 MG100171 ## 2 MG100198 -0.164123841 0.04221565 0.02443569 MG100174 ## 3 MG100199 -0.034656783 0.06907268 -0.09141366 MG100175 ## 4 MG100200 -0.037125834 0.08835104 -0.01446685 MG100176 ## 5 MG100201 0.0102459480 0.09353651 0.11426126 MG100177 ## 6 MG100202 0.002223516 0.10249228 0.02265875 MG100178

Start plotting the figures. The legends will show up on top of the figures here, but running the first plot without the second legend line will generate the plot alone (this is what was used in publication) The legends will show up on top of the figures here, but running the first plot without the second legend line will generate the plot alone (this is what was used in publication) s3d s3d <- scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), type="h") legend('right', pch = 16,legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), perm=999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories) 2 2.562 1.28109 5.0118 Residuals 249 63.649 0.25562 Total 251 66.211 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Plot time point. s3d s3d <- scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint)), type="h") legend('right', pch = 16,legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint)), inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint), perm=999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint) 1 2.279 2.27899 8.9118 Residuals 250 63.932 0.25573 Total 251 66.211 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Plot ordination for occlusion site status. s3d s3d <- scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2,NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch=16, color=as.integer(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), type="h") legend('right', pch = 16,legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), inset=c(0.1,0)) adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), perm=999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$TimePoint) 1 2.279 2.27899 8.9118 Residuals 250 63.932 0.25573 Total 251 66.211 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
We will also perform the negative control ordination to validate we have samples over background, which are significantly different from the rest of the samples.

Environmental background contol in the analysis Generate subset of mapping file for only the specific anatomic sites and all time points (2 and 3). SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[-which(INPUT_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID %in% NA), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[which(SUBSET_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2, 3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba", "Ph", "Vf")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2, 3, 9, 11)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[c(order(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID)), ]

This will define each sample as a control or not: for (i in 1:length(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories)) { if (SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories[i] == for (i in 1:length(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories)) { if (SUBSET_MAP$Site_Categories[i] == "Control") { SUBSET_MAP$CNTRL[i] = "Cntrl" } else { SUBSET_MAP$CNTRL[i] = "Smpl" } } SUBSET_MAP$CNTRL <- factor(SUBSET_MAP$CNTRL)

Get only the samples described in the map subset. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_NO_FINAL[which(INPUT_NO_FINAL$ContigID %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] row.names(INPUT_SUBSET) <- INPUT_SUBSET[, 1] INPUT_SUB_FORMAT <- INPUT_SUBSET[, -1]
Generate distance matrix using Bray Curtis for all time points (2 and 3). INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX

Visualize the distance matrix using NMDS. BRAY_ORD_NMDS ## Run 0 stress 0.2106771 ## Run 1 stress 0.2152638 ## Run 2 stress 0.2212825 ## Run 3 stress 0.2131834 ## Run 4 stress 0.2137311 ## Run 5 stress 0.2188144 ## Run 6 stress 0.2098836 ## ... New best solution ## ... procrustes: rmse 0.02215487 max resid 0.2231707 ## Run 7 stress 0.2292835 ## Run 8 stress 0.218468 ## Run 9 stress 0.2248454 ## Run 10 stress 0.2147353 ## Run 11 stress 0.2143595 ## Run 12 stress 0.2191855 ## Run 13 stress 0.2148725 ## Run 14 stress 0.2248317 ## Run 15 stress 0.2357207 ## Run 16 stress 0.2133108 ## Run 17 stress 0.217102 ## Run 18 stress 0.2149535 ## Run 19 stress 0.210627 ## Run 20 stress 0.2231295

Record the stress value. BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 2])BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 2]) BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress ## [1] 0.2098836

Plot MDS1 vs MDS2. RAY_ORD_FIT$SampleID RAY_ORD_FIT$SampleID <- rownames(BRAY_ORD_FIT) NMDS_AND_MAP <- merge(BRAY_ORD_FIT, SUBSET_MAP, by.x = "SampleID", by.y = "NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") ggplot(NMDS_AND_MAP, aes(x = MDS1, y = MDS2, size = 6, group = CNTRL, colour = CNTRL)) + theme_bw() + geom_point()

Calculate the significance of the differences between the environmental background and the rest of the samples. adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$CNTRL), perm = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$CNTRL) 1 5.439 5.4391 21.422 Residuals 282 71.601 0.2539 Total 283 77.040 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
In order to evaluate the utility of reference-independent methods, we also calculated alpha and beta diversity metrics using our reference-dependent taxonomic data. INPUT_SPECIES
Also import the mapping file. INPUT_MAP

Subset the mapping file. SUBSET_MAP SUBSET_MAP <- INPUT_MAP[which(INPUT_MAP$TimePoint %in% c(2, 3)), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba", "Ph", "Vf", "Neg")), ] SUBSET_MAP <- SUBSET_MAP[-which(SUBSET_MAP$SubjectID %in% c(2, 3, 9, 11)), ]

Same formatting and parsing as above. From here we would calculate the mean across the various sites and merge in the mapping file data but we are going to want to use this file here for distance matrix calculations. KEEP_SAMPLES KEEP_SAMPLES <- as.vector(SUBSET_MAP$NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID) INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_SPECIES[which(INPUT_SPECIES$V3 %in% c(KEEP_SAMPLES)), ] INPUT_SUBSET <- INPUT_SUBSET[-which(INPUT_SUBSET$V1 %in% c("No_hit")), ]
Convert the file to wide format so that it can be used for distance matrix calculations. inputWide

I pulled out a couple values to ensure the transformation was correct. inputWideTranspose inputWideTranspose <- as.data.frame(t(inputWide[, -1])) INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX <- vegdist(inputWideTranspose, method = "bray")

Visualize the distance matrix using NMDS. BRAY_ORD_NMDS ## Run 0 stress 0.08479484 ## Run 1 stress 0.08752696 ## Run 2 stress 0.0945716 ## Run 3 stress 0.09044704 ## Run 4 stress 0.09281868 ## Run 5 stress 0.09634914 ## Run 6 stress 0.09194107 ## Run 7 stress 0.09115468 ## Run 8 stress 0.08718591 ## Run 9 stress 0.09208599 ## Run 10 stress 0.08650345 ## Run 11 stress 0.09094712 ## Run 12 stress 0.09453678 ## Run 13 stress 0.09349933 ## Run 14 stress 0.08866572 ## Run 15 stress 0.09333028 ## Run 16 stress 0.09083254 ## Run 17 stress 0.08825317 ## Run 18 stress 0.0877584 ## Run 19 stress 0.09264528 ## Run 20 stress 0.09402247

Plot the data. BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 2], MDS3 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 3])BRAY_ORD_NMDS_STRESS BRAY_ORD_FIT = data.frame(MDS1 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 1], MDS2 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 2], MDS3 = BRAY_ORD_NMDS$points[, 3]) BRAY_ORD_NMDS_STRESS <- BRAY_ORD_NMDS$stress NMDS_AND_MAP <- merge(BRAY_ORD_FIT, SUBSET_MAP, by.x = "row.names", by.y = "NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") s3d <- scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1, NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2, NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch = 16, color = as.integer(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), type = "h") legend("right", pch = 16, legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories)), inset = c(0.1, 0))

Test the significance. adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), perm = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories) 2 0.6099 0.304959 3.8099 Residuals 249 19.9311 0.080045 Total 251 20.5410 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Also plot occlusion. s3d s3d <- scatterplot3d(NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS1, NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS2, NMDS_AND_MAP$MDS3, pch = 16, color = as.integer(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), type = "h") legend("right", pch = 16, legend = levels(factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), col = seq_along(levels(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion)), inset = c(0.1, 0))

Test the significance. There is a significant difference between the sites, even when using the taxonomic profiles, suggests that even though the annotated portion of the data only represents a small portion of the community, it is actually able to detect the large trends for the overall community. adonis(INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion), perm = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Call: ## adonis(formula = INPUT_SUBSET_DIST_MATRIX ~ factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Site_Categories), permutations = 999, strata = factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$SubjectID)) ## ## Blocks: strata ## Permutation: free ## Number of permutations: 999 ## ## Terms added sequentially (first to last) ## %s %s Df SumsOfSqs MeansSqs F.Model factor(NMDS_AND_MAP$Occlusion) 2 0.6008 0.300423 3.7515 Residuals 249 19.9402 0.080081 Total 251 20.5410 ## Residuals ## Total ## --- ## Signif. codes: 0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Also look at the alpha diversity. shannonDiv shannonDiv <- as.data.frame(diversity(inputWideTranspose, index = "shannon")) colnames(shannonDiv) <- c("shannonDiv") alphaAndMap <- merge(shannonDiv, SUBSET_MAP, by.x = "row.names", by.y = "NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID") MicroEnvPlot <- ggplot(alphaAndMap, aes(x = Site_Categories, y = shannonDiv)) + theme_classic() + geom_boxplot(notch = TRUE) MicroEnvPlot

Check for significance of differences between the groups. Check levels of categories. alphaAndMap$Site_Categories alphaAndMap$Site_Categories <- factor(alphaAndMap$Site_Categories) levels(alphaAndMap$Site_Categories) ## [1] "Intermittently_Moist" "Moist" "Sebaceous"

Run Kruskal-Wallis on the dataset. kruskalmc(alphaAndMap$shannonDiv, alphaAndMap$Site_Categories) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Intermittently_Moist-Moist 25.987989 28.54913 FALSE Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 8.339718 28.43069 FALSE Moist-Sebaceous 17.648271 25.32023 FALSE
Also look at occlusion status. OcclusionPlot OcclusionPlot <- ggplot(alphaAndMap, aes(x = Occlusion, y = shannonDiv)) + theme_classic() + geom_boxplot(notch = TRUE) OcclusionPlot

Check for significance of differences between groups. Check levels of categories. alphaAndMap$Occlusion alphaAndMap$Occlusion <- factor(alphaAndMap$Occlusion) levels(alphaAndMap$Occlusion) ## [1] "Exposed" "Intermittently_Occluded" ## [3] "Occluded" ## [1] "Exposed" "Intermittently_Occluded" ## [3] "Occluded" ## [1] "Exposed" "Intermittently_Occluded" ## [3] "Occluded"

Run Kruskal-Wallis on the dataset. ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 13.804754 36.09383 FALSE Exposed-Occluded 20.761487 23.71043 FALSE Intermittently_Occluded-Occluded 6.959733 34.98437 FALSE
creator: Geoffrey D Hannigan
